title: lecanorchis multiflora var. bihuensis
intro: Lecanorchis multiflora var. bihuensis, also known as the Bihu orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of the Philippines. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is valued for its potential to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The Bihu orchid is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, contributing to its health benefits. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is prepared in various ways, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, from fever and cough to digestive issues and skin conditions.
